mechanism_narrative: A lower FEDFUNDS level transmits through discount-rate relief and easier financial conditions, lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average when FEDFUNDS is falling or remains at cycle lows, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rise in RSAFS above recent trend signals stronger consumer demand and revenue momentum, which should lift near-term equity prices over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month change turns positive and exceeds its prior 6-month median
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations fail to show a positive average 20-day SPX return, or if the hit rate is below 50% across at least 30 triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above its recent baseline indicates weakening labor income and demand, which should pressure cyclically sensitive equity earnings and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not negative on average after 0.3pp/3-month unemployment increases, or if the sign flips positive in more than 60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Higher INDPRO together with lower UNRATE identifies improving production with tight labor absorption; this combination signals stronger real activity and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint state of rising INDPRO and falling UNRATE does not outperform either proxy alone, or if the interaction term is not positive and significant in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is accelerating while FEDFUNDS remains near zero, real policy rates fall further negative and liquidity expectations become supportive of risk assets, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds FEDFUNDS by at least 2 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if the inflation-minus-policy-rate trigger does not precede positive 5-day SPX returns more often than chance, or if average returns are non-positive in the triggered state.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST signals a housing-demand slowdown that leads broad consumption and growth expectations down, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 8% from its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day SPX returns are not negative after large HOUST drawdowns, or if the signal loses predictive power once minimum sample size exceeds 25 triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO with stable or declining CPIAUCSL indicates real output growth without inflation pressure, which should support equity multiples and earnings expectations over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combination of higher INDPRO and flat-or-falling CPIAUCSL does not produce positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the interaction effect is indistinguishable from zero in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a labor-market deterioration regime, a simultaneous fall in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE confirms broad payroll loss and weaker household income, which should generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint payroll decline and unemployment rise fails to predict negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the effect disappears when PAYEMS revisions are accounted for.
